name: Karaoke GS Studio
tagline: Japanese-style private room karaoke open til 4am — 16 rooms, full bar, and a local pastime taken seriously.
parking: Oasis Hotel parking or nearby Waikiki paid lots
bestTime: Pau hana (after work) or late night on weekends — book rooms ahead for groups
talkStory: Karaoke in Hawaii isn't an ironic tourist activity — it's a genuine local pastime, as central to social life as a backyard BBQ. GS Studio in the Oasis Hotel on Lewers St does it right: 16 private rooms (fitting 2-30 people), Japanese-style with proper audio systems, a full bar with food service, and rooms starting around $10/hour. The song library covers Asian and English hits. Locals book rooms for birthdays, pau hana gatherings, and weekend blowouts. The 4am closing time means karaoke nights that start at 10pm can properly go the distance.
